sections.title: Fats
sections.narration: 3.3g of fat. cholesterol, saturated fat, and polyunsaturated fat are doing most of the work. for dairy, the fat side can either add richness or drag the whole thing down.
sections.subtitleText: 3.3g of fat. cholesterol, saturated fat, and polyunsaturated fat are doing most of the work. for dairy, the fat side can either add richness or drag the whole thing down.

sections.title: Carbs
sections.narration: 4.7g of carbs. sugar and glycemic index matter most here. for dairy, the best versions keep the convenience without wrecking the fat profile.
sections.subtitleText: 4.7g of carbs. sugar and glycemic index matter most here. for dairy, the best versions keep the convenience without wrecking the fat profile.

sections.title: Proteins
sections.narration: 3.5g of protein. bioavailability and amino acid quality are both strong. useful protein can rescue a lot of weaker traits here.
sections.subtitleText: 3.5g of protein. bioavailability and amino acid quality are both strong. useful protein can rescue a lot of weaker traits here.

sections.title: Vitamins
sections.narration: vitamin b12 at 18% daily value stands out most. that is barely moving the needle.
sections.subtitleText: vitamin b12 at 18% daily value stands out most. that is barely moving the needle.

sections.title: Minerals
sections.narration: calcium at 12% daily value stands out most. useful, but still pretty limited.
sections.subtitleText: calcium at 12% daily value stands out most. useful, but still pretty limited.

sections.title: Pros
sections.narration: pros first: easy to fit into familiar meals because that lowers friction for regular use. easy protein delivery for normal diets because dairy gets real credit for convenience. clear calcium identity because that is a core category strength.

sections.title: Cons
sections.narration: on the weaker side: tolerance varies a lot person to person because a category strength is less useful if the food does not agree with you. processing changes the category a lot because the label alone does not tell the whole story. not everyone tolerates dairy well because that is a real use-case limitation.

closing.summary: Plain Yogurt has a genuinely strong overall case. the practical downsides are real, but they do not outweigh the upside. best when the protein payoff or fermentation angle outweighs the fat and sodium tradeoffs.
closing.useCaseNote: Best when the protein payoff or fermentation angle outweighs the fat and sodium tradeoffs.
closing.cta: Would you rank it the same, or nah?

explanation.summary: Proteins are carrying this food most, while minerals are the biggest drag. It lands in A tier.
